Inclusive Growth, Societal & Environment.description: Stakeholders should proactively engage in responsible stewardship of trustworthy AI in pursuit of beneficial outcomes for people and the planet, such as augmenting human capabilities and enhancing creativity, advancing inclusion of underrepresented populations, reducing economic, social, gender, and other inequalities, and protecting natural environments, thus invigorating inclusive growth, sustainable development, and well-being.

testable_criteria.text: Ensure that the development of AI system is for the beneficial outcomes for individuals, society, and the environment

process_checks.process_text: Put in place a process to determine that the development and deployment of the AI system is for the benefit of people, society, and the environment, where applicable
process_checks.check_description:
Documentary evidence of consideration of AI system's impact on individuals, society, and environment, which may include (where applicable):
- Human capabilities to learn and make decisions
- Skills, jobs, and/or job quality
- Creative economies
- Discriminatory and/or exclusionary norms
- Environmental concerns

all_process_checks.process_text: Put in place a process to determine that the development and deployment of the AI system is for the benefit of people, society, and the environment, where applicable
all_process_checks.check_description:
Documentary evidence of consideration of AI system's impact on individuals, society, and environment, which may include (where applicable):
- Human capabilities to learn and make decisions
- Skills, jobs, and/or job quality
- Creative economies
- Discriminatory and/or exclusionary norms
- Environmental concerns

Accountability.description: Accountability is about having clear internal governance mechanisms for proper management oversight of the AI system's development and deployment.

all_process_checks.criteria_text: Establish clear internal governance mechanisms to ensure clear roles and responsibilities for the use of AI by the organisation
all_process_checks.process_text: Adapt existing structures, communication lines, procedures, and rules (e.g., three lines of defense risk management model) or implement new ones
all_process_checks.check_description: Documentary evidence of adaptation or new implementation of structures, communication lines, procedures, and rules (e.g., three lines of defense risk management model)

all_process_checks.criteria_text: Establish clear internal governance mechanisms to ensure clear roles and responsibilities for the use of AI by the organisation
all_process_checks.process_text: For organisations who are using AI across departments, establish an AI governance committee that comprises representatives from data science, technology, risk, and product to facilitate cross-departmental oversight for the lifecycle governance of AI systems
all_process_checks.check_description:
Documentary evidence of the establishment of an AI governance committee.

This committee should be sufficiently representative. One way to achieve this is by having representatives from:
- data science;
- technology;
- legal and compliance;
- risk and product; and
- user experience research, ethics, and psychology

all_process_checks.criteria_text: Establish clear internal governance mechanisms to ensure clear roles and responsibilities for the use of AI by the organisation
all_process_checks.process_text: Enable a process to report on actions or decisions that affect the AI system's outcome, and a corresponding process for the accountable party to respond to the consequences of such an outcome
all_process_checks.check_description:
Documentary evidence that outlines roles, responsibilities, and key processes for

- the reporting on actions or decisions that affect the AI system's outcome;
- the corresponding process for the accountable party to respond to the consequences of such an outcome

all_process_checks.criteria_text: Establish the appropriate process or governance-by-design technology to automate or facilitate the AI system’s auditability throughout its lifecycle
all_process_checks.process_text:
Process or technology should handle:
- Version control of code and model
- Version data or maintain immutable data
- Audit trail of deployment history, log inputs/outputs, associate server predictions with the originating model
all_process_checks.check_description:
Documentary evidence of the establishment of the appropriate process or governance-by-design technology to automate or facilitate the AI system’s auditability throughout its lifecycle.

The process or technology should handle:
- Version control of code and model;
- Version data or maintain immutable data; and
- Audit trail of deployment history, log inputs/outputs, associate server predictions with the originating model

all_process_checks.criteria_text: Define the policy mechanism for enforcing access rights and permissions for the various roles of users
all_process_checks.process_text:
Implement fine-grained access control that aligns with various roles for users:
- Access to code and data for training AI models
- Access to code and data for deploying AI models
- Access to different execution environments
- Permission to perform various actions (e.g., launch training job, review model, deploy model server)
- Permission to define access control rules and perform other administrative functions
all_process_checks.check_description:
Documentary evidence of the implementation of fine-grained access control that aligns with various roles for users, which include:

- Access to code and data for training AI models
- Access to code and data for deploying AI models
- Access to different execution environments
- Permission to perform various actions (e.g., launch training job, review model, deploy model server)
- Permission to define access control rules and perform other administrative functions

all_process_checks.criteria_text: Establish a strategy for maintaining independent oversight over the development and deployment of AI systems
all_process_checks.process_text: Reviewers should be distinct from those who are training and deploying models. However, it is acceptable to have the same individuals training and deploying models
all_process_checks.metric: Internal documentation (e.g., log, register or database)
all_process_checks.check_description: Documentary evidence of strategy for maintaining independent oversight over the development and deployment of AI systems

all_process_checks.criteria_text: If you are using third-party ‘black box’ models, assess the suitability and limits of the model for your use case
all_process_checks.process_text: Evaluate the necessity of third-party models e.g., they are trained on data otherwise not accessible to your organisation ,or you do not have the requisite capability to build AI systems in-house
all_process_checks.check_description: Documentary evidence of evaluation completed regarding the necessity of third-party models

all_process_checks.criteria_text: If you are using third-party ‘black box’ models, assess the suitability and limits of the model for your use case
all_process_checks.process_text: Demonstrate effort to understand how the third-party models were built, including 1) what data was used to train the models, 2) how the models are assessed for effectiveness and explainability 3) under what circumstances does the AI system perform poorly
all_process_checks.check_description:
Documentary evidence of effort undertaken to understand how the third-party models were built, which includes:

- what data was used to train the models;
- how the models are assessed for effectiveness and explainability; and
- under what circumstances does the AI system perform poorly

all_process_checks.criteria_text: Establish clear responsibilities between different parties within the broader supply chain – partners, suppliers, customers, third parties
all_process_checks.process_text: Responsibilities and obligations are clearly communicated to all parties related to the AI system
all_process_checks.check_description: Documentary evidence of communication with relevant parties on responsibilities and obligations relating to the AI system

all_process_checks.criteria_text: Establish processes to ensure that its usage of services, products or materials are in support of the responsible development and use of the AI system
all_process_checks.process_text:
Select AI system suppliers which align with organisation’s approach.

When AI system suppliers do not perform as intended, there are processes for suppliers to take remedial actions.
all_process_checks.check_description:
Documentary evidence of processes to select suppliers which align with the organisation’s approach.

Documentary evidence of correspondence with suppliers to take corrective action.

all_process_checks.criteria_text: Incorporate end users’ expectations/needs in the responsible development and use of the AI system
all_process_checks.process_text: Process to engage end users on their expectations and needs (e.g. providing general usage agreements that scope its use)
all_process_checks.check_description: Documentary evidence of attempts to understand end users needs and mitigate risks related to its misuse

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Provide the necessary information to end users about the use of their personal data to ensure it is processed in a fair and transparent manner
all_checks_flat.process_text: Align with (1) the PDPC’s Advisory Guidelines on Key Concepts in the PDPA; (2) Guide to Accountability; and (3) Guide to Data Protection Impact Assessments
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of internal policy requiring alignment with existing data protection laws and regulations, which include:
(in Singapore)
- PDPC’s Advisory Guidelines on Key Concepts in the PDPA;
- Guide to Accountability; and
- Guide to Data Protection Impact Assessments.
(outside Singapore)
- Applicable data protection laws/regulations

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Provide the necessary information to end users about the use of their personal data to ensure it is processed in a fair and transparent manner
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Publish a privacy policy on your organization’s website to share information about the use of personal data in the AI system (e.g., data practices, and decision-making processes). The general disclosure notice could include:
– Disclosure of third-party engagement
– Definition of data ownership and portability
– Depiction of the data flow and identify any leakages
– Identification of standards the company is compliant with as assurance to customers
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of a privacy policy on your organization’s website to share information about the use of personal data in the AI system (e.g., data practices and decision-making processes).

The general disclosure notice could include:
– Disclosure of third-party engagement;
– Definition of data ownership and portability;
– Depiction of the data flow and identify any leakages; and
– Identification of standards the company is compliant with as assurance to customers

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Where possible (e.g., not compromising IP, safety, or system integrity), identify appropriate junctures in the AI lifecycle to inform end users, subjects and other relevant parties about necessary information regarding the AI system (e.g., purpose, criteria, limitations and risks of the decision(s) generated by the AI system) in an accessible manner
all_checks_flat.process_text: Design an in-house policy on communication to consumers that articulates the principles for transparency, e.g., define the purpose and context of communication to determine how and what to communicate
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of an in-house policy on communication to consumers that articulates the principles for transparency, e.g., define the purpose and context of communication to determine how and what to communicate

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Where possible (e.g., not compromising IP, safety, or system integrity), identify appropriate junctures in the AI lifecycle to inform end users, subjects and other relevant parties about necessary information regarding the AI system (e.g., purpose, criteria, limitations and risks of the decision(s) generated by the AI system) in an accessible manner
all_checks_flat.process_text: Inform relevant stakeholders that AI is used in your products and/or services
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of communication to relevant stakeholders that AI is used in the organisation's products and/or services

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Where possible (e.g., not compromising IP, safety, or system integrity), identify appropriate junctures in the AI lifecycle to inform end users, subjects and other relevant parties about necessary information regarding the AI system (e.g., purpose, criteria, limitations and risks of the decision(s) generated by the AI system) in an accessible manner
all_checks_flat.process_text: For decisions made by the AI system, where possible, communicate to end users the factors leading to the decision e.g., "You are being shown this product because you bought this item."
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of communicating to end users the factors that lead to decisions made by AI systems

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Where possible (e.g., not compromising IP, safety, or system integrity), identify appropriate junctures in the AI lifecycle to inform end users, subjects and other relevant parties about necessary information regarding the AI system (e.g., purpose, criteria, limitations and risks of the decision(s) generated by the AI system) in an accessible manner
all_checks_flat.process_text: Consult end users at the earliest stages of AI system development to communicate how the technology is used and how it will be deployed
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of communication with end users at early stages of AI system development concerning how the technology is used and how it will be deployed

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Where possible (e.g., not compromising IP, safety, or system integrity), identify appropriate junctures in the AI lifecycle to inform end users, subjects and other relevant parties about necessary information regarding the AI system (e.g., purpose, criteria, limitations and risks of the decision(s) generated by the AI system) in an accessible manner
all_checks_flat.process_text: Surface relevant information (e.g., regarding accuracy, intended use cases, and limitations of the AI system, including the risk assessment), to stakeholders such as end users and relevant authorities in alignment with regulations
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of communication with stakeholders concerning the AI system, which includes (where applicable):

- accuracy;
- confidence scores;
- intended use cases;
- limitations; and
- risk assessment

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Provide information to guide end users on the proper use of the AI system in an accessible manner
all_checks_flat.process_text: Provide information such as the purpose, intended use and intended response of the AI system to end users
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of communication with end users concerning the intended use and intended response of the AI system (e.g., Model Card and Data Card)

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Provide end users and other external parties with the means to report adverse impacts of AI system to the organisation
all_checks_flat.process_text: Processes in place for reporting of adverse impacts of AI system
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of processes by which end users and other external parties are able to report adverse impacts of AI sytem

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Communicate to end users whenever an incident has occurred
all_checks_flat.process_text: Processes in place for communication of incidents to end users
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of communication plan

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Demonstrate a preference for developing AI models that can explain their decisions or that are interpretable by default
all_checks_flat.process_text: If choosing a less explainable modelling approach, document the rationale, risk assessments, and trade-offs of the AI model
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of considerations for the choice of AI model

Considerations include:
- rationale;
- risk assessment; and
- trade-offs

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place methods to record the provenance of the AI model, including the various versions, configurations, data transformations, and underlying source code
all_checks_flat.process_text: Implement version control of source code and frameworks used to develop the model. For each version of the model, track the code version, as well as the parameters, hyperparameters, and source data used
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of version control of source code and frameworks used to develop the model, including considerations of how much version history is required

Each version of the model should track the following:
- code version;
- parameters;
- hyperparameters; and
- source data

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place measures to ensure data quality over time
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Verify the quality of data used in the AI system. This may include the following:
- accuracy in terms of how well the values in the dataset match the true characteristics of the entity described by the dataset
- completeness in terms of attributes and items e.g., checking for missing values, duplicate records
- veracity in terms of how credible the data is, including whether the data originated from a reliable source
- How recently the dataset was compiled or updated
- Relevance for the intended purpose
- Integrity in terms of how well extraction and transformation have been performed if multiple datasets are joined;
- Usability in terms of how the data are tracked and stored in a consistent, human-readable format
- Providing distribution analysis e.g., feature distributions of input data
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that proves due diligence has been done to ensure the quality of data. This can include the use of relevant processes or software that:
- Conducts validation schema checks
- Identifies possible errors and inconsistencies at the exploratory data analysis stage, before training the dataset
- Assigns roles to the entire data pipeline to trace who manipulated data and by which rule
- Allows for review before a change is made
- Unit tests to validate that each data operation is performed correctly prior to deployment
- Allow for periodic reviewing and update of datasets
- Allow for continuous assessment of the quality of the input data to the AI system, including drift parameters and thresholds, where applicable

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place measures to understand the lineage of data, including knowing where the data originally came from, how it was collected, curated, and moved within the organisation over time
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Maintain a data provenance record to ascertain the quality of the data based on its origin and subsequent transformation. This could include the following:
- Take steps to understand the meaning of and how data was collected
- Document data usage and related concerns.
- Ensure any data labeling is done by a representative group of labelers
- Document the procedure for assessing labels for bias
- Trace potential sources of errors
-Update data
- Attribute data to their sources
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of a data provenance record that includes the following info, where applicable:
- clear explanations of what data is used, how it is collected, and why
- source of data and its labels
- who the labelers were and whether bias tests were conducted to assess if the labelled data was biased (e.g., bias assessment)
- how data is transformed over time
- risk management if the origin of data is difficult to be established

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Trace the data used by the AI system to make a certain decision(s) or recommendation(s)
all_checks_flat.process_text: Log and capture clearly the data used to train a model version, as well as produce inference results using the model (batch scoring or API endpoint)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of data used.

Data (raw and synthetic data) includes:
- data used to train the AI model;
- data used to produce inference results using the AI model (batch scoring or API endpoint)

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Trace the AI model or rules that led to the decision(s) or recommendation(s) of the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text: Link the inference results of the model (batch scoring or API endpoint) back to the underlying model and source code
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of linking the inference results of the model (batch scoring or API endpoint) back to the underlying model and source code

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place adequate logging practices to record the decision(s) or recommendation(s) of the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text: Log all inputs and inference outputs of the model, and store them for a reasonable duration so that they can be reviewed
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of log records covering all inputs and inference outputs of the model.

Log records would cover:
- decisions(s) of AI system; and/or
- recommendation(s) of the AI system
- if a human accepted or rejected AI recommendations/decisions, especially when human-in-the-loop is required

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Reproduce the training process for every evaluated model (except data)
all_checks_flat.process_text: Version control model artefacts by associating each artefact with the version of code, dependencies, and parameters used in training
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of version control model artefacts.

Each artefact includes:
- version of code
- dependencies; and
- parameters used in training

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Assess for repeatability by reviewing if the model produces the same output based on the same input  (Note: this is not relevant when it's time to the retrain model)
all_checks_flat.process_text: Calculate multiple inferences. If the data follows a normal distribution, the accepted limits of this difference (or 95% of it at least) are +/-1.96 times the standard deviation of the differences between the means of the two tests
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of assessment conducted to review if the model produces the same output based on the same input

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Define the process for developing models and evaluate the process
all_checks_flat.process_text: Identify a combination of technical metrics and business metrics that AI models are designed to be assessed against
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of metrics of AI models that are designed to be assessed against.

Metrics include:
- technical metrics; and/or
- business metrics

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Define the process for developing models and evaluate the process
all_checks_flat.process_text: Keep track of experiments (e.g., hyperparameters and model performance) used to develop challenger models, document the rationale for developing these models, and how the final deployed model was arrived at
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of the process in developing the AI model.

The process includes:
- hyperparameters, model performance, and other relevant aspects used to develop challenger models;
- the rationale for developing these models; and
- how the final deployed model was derived

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a strategy for reproducing the input data used in the training process for every model
all_checks_flat.process_text: Version control the input data used for training where possible. If not possible, avoid changing the raw data at the source, and keep track of the various stages or transformation steps that are part of the data pipeline for AI model development, preferably as a directed acyclic graph (DAG)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of having implemented a strategy for reproducing the input data used in the training process for every model.

This strategy includes:
- data cleaning, data processing, and feature engineering
- maintaining version control of the input data used for training the AI model, where possible; or
- separating data manipulation process into extraction (data versioning) and processing; or
- avoiding changes to the raw data at the source and keeping track of the various stages or transformation steps that are part of the data pipeline for AI model development, preferably as a directed acyclic graph (DAG).

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a strategy for ensuring that assumptions still hold across subsequent model retraining process on new input data
all_checks_flat.process_text: Record the statistical distribution of input features and output results so that divergence during retraining can be flagged. Monitor input parameters and evaluation metrics for anomalies across retraining runs
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of establishing a strategy for ensuring that assumptions still hold across subsequent model retraining process on new input data. For example:
- K-L divergence and K-S test metrics can be used to compare the statistical distributions of inputs/outputs between two training runs
- Moving average and standard deviations can be used to detect a significant change in model performance metrics

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Reproduce outputs of the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text: Log audit trail of when and how each model was deployed, including the code used to serve the model, testing/validation data, and what version of the model artefact was used
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of past outputs of deployed AI system, which can include:
- when and how each model was deployed;
- the code used to serve the model; and
- the version of the model artefact used

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: If using a blackbox model or third party model, assess the vendor's claim on accuracy
all_checks_flat.process_text: Curate the test set and apply the test set on the model to review performance
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of assessment conducted concerning vendor's claim on the accuracy, if using a blackbox or third party model

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a strategy to continuously assess the quality of the output(s) of the AI system and ensure that the operating conditions of a live AI system match the thesis under which it was originally developed
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Continuous monitoring and periodic validation should be conducted even after models have gone live. This includes:
- Model performance, e.g., monitor feature drift, inference drift, accuracy against ground truth
- Application performance, e.g,, latency, throughput, error rates
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of the conduct of continuous monitoring and periodic validation even after models have gone live.

This can include:
- Notifications to admins when a model/system exceeds some thresholds and the system is paused (if safe to do so) until the model can be improved. Any decisions that have been made/implemented while the AI was below a threshold should be flagged for reevaluation and potentially redress/remediation if harm occurred
- Model performance (e.g.,monitor feature drift, inference drift, accuracy against ground truth)
- Application performance (e.g., latency, throughput, error rates)

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Carry out an assessment of materiality on key stakeholders
all_checks_flat.process_text: Complete and submit the Assessment of Materiality to the appropriate parties who are accountable for the AI system (e.g., AI governance committee, AI system owner, and reviewers) and highlight the risks of the proposed AI solution. Document the justifications for decisions on materiality and the application of relevant governance and controls to demonstrate to regulators and auditors that sufficient responsibility has been taken by humans to address potential risks. The materality assessment could be based on expected use, past uses of AI systems in similar contexts, public incident reports, feedback from those external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system, or other data
all_checks_flat.metric: 1) Internal procedure manual 2) Internal documentation (e.g., procedure manual)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of details of the assessment of materiality on key stakeholders, justifications for decisions on materiality, and the application of relevant governance/controls.

The Assessment of Materiality includes the following impact dimensions (where applicable):
- probability of harm;
- severity of harm;
- Likelihood of threat;
- Extent of human involvement;
- Complexity of AI model;
- Extensiveness of impact on stakeholders;
- Degree of Transparency; and
- Impact on trust

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Assess risks, risk metrics, and risk levels of the AI system in each specific use case, including the dependency of a critical AI system’s decisions on its stable and reliable behaviour
all_checks_flat.process_text: Document the intended use cases, capabilities, and limitations of AI models e.g., via model cards. This documentation should be stored and retrieved together with the model artefact, as well as surfaced during a review process before the model is deployed into production
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of risk assessment done for specific use cases.

This risk assessment includes documenting* the:
- intended use cases, capabilities, and limitations of the AI model (e.g., via model cards)

*Note: This documentation should be stored and retrieved together with the model artefact and surfaced during a review process before the model is deployed into production

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place a process to continuously assess, measure and monitor risks, including the identification of new risks after deployment
all_checks_flat.process_text: Assign a reviewer who is familiar with the downstream use case of an AI model to review the model post-deployment. This process should include model cards/documentation to ensure alignment between intended use cases at modelling and post-deployment
all_checks_flat.metric: Internal documentation (e.g., log, register or database)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of process for continuous risk monitoring for AI model.

Process includes:
- Assessing, measuring, and monitoring risks at modelling stage; and
- identification of new risks after the post-deployment stage

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Assess whether the AI system might fail by considering the input features and predicted outcomes to aid communication with stakeholders
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Where feasible, use AI models that can produce confidence score together with prediction. Low confidence scores may occur when the data contains values that are outside the range of the training data, or for data regions where there were insufficient training examples to make a robust estimate.
Implement mechanisms to detect if model input represents an outlier in terms of training data, e.g., return some "data outlier score" with predictions
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of assessment of whether the AI system might fail by considering the input features and predicted outcomes to aid communication to stakeholders

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Plan fault tolerance via, e.g., a duplicated system or another parallel system (AI-based or ‘conventional’)
all_checks_flat.process_text: Implement deployment strategies such as blue-green and canary deployments.
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of:
- implementation of deployment strategies such as blue-green and canary deployments
- a plan for graceful failure or failover modes

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Plan fault tolerance via, e.g., a duplicated system or another parallel system (AI-based or ‘conventional’)
all_checks_flat.process_text: Maintain backup model server in blue-green deployment mode.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of maintenance of the backup model server in blue-green deployment mode

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Plan fault tolerance via, e.g., a duplicated system or another parallel system (AI-based or ‘conventional’)
all_checks_flat.process_text: Where feasible, use AI models that can produce a confidence score together with the prediction. Design the systems that are using the results of the AI model to handle cases where the model fails or has low confidence, falling back to backup model servers or sensible default behaviour.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of the use of AI models that can produce a confidence score together with the prediction, and that it can fall back to backup model servers or sensible default behaviour

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Plan fault tolerance via, e.g., a duplicated system or another parallel system (AI-based or ‘conventional’)
all_checks_flat.process_text: Close the feedback loop by retraining models with ground truth obtained once models are in production.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of closing the feedback loop by retraining models with ground truth obtained once models are in production

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Identify residual risk that cannot be mitigated and assess the organisation's tolerance for these risks
all_checks_flat.process_text: Document the assessment of the residual risk and provide reasons for the tolerance level
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of assessment of residual risk and the reasons for the organisation's tolerance for these risks

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Ensure Team Competency
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Ensure that relevant team members are knowledgeable about threats, vulnerabilities, impact, and mitigation measures relevant to securing AI systems and that their knowledge is up to date

Relevant team members may include any employee that is involved in the model lifecycle
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that team members have relevant security knowledge and training on threats, vulnerabilities, impact, and mitigation measures relevant to securing AI systems. This can include, where applicable:
- Training records
- Attendance records
- Assessments
- Certifications
- Feedback forms

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Conduct security risk assessment at the Inception of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text: Ensure that proper risk assessment has been carried out, in accordance with the relevant industry standards. Risk mitigation steps have been taken
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that risk assessment has been done in accordance with the relevant industry standards/guidelines/best practices, with risk mitigation steps and factors taken. This can include:
- US NIST AI Risk Management Framework
- UK NCSC guidance on secure development and deployment of software applications
- OWASP Secure Software Development Lifecycle (SSDLC)
- CIA triad

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures during the Verification and Validation of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text: Ensure there is integrity in data and/or models and there is a chain of custody
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that data and/or models have been obtained from a trusted source. If unable to obtain data from a trusted source, document the reason and process for using synthetic or limited data. This can include practices implemented according to:
- UK NCSC supply chain security guidance
- ETSI GR SAI 002 Securing AI Data Supply Chain Security
- UK DSTL Machine Learning with Limited Data

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures during the Verification and Validation of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text: Assess the integrity of acquired datasets with a robust validation and verification process
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of assessment of the integrity of acquired datasets with a robust validation and verification process:
- For internal labelled data: Have multiple labellers look at each data input and generate notification where labels differ
- External procured/created data: Where possible, follow NCSC supply chain security guidance to find a trusted vendor
- Randomized audits of data labels to assess error rates

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures during the Design and Development of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text: Ensure that the development environment has been secured, including trust access controls
all_checks_flat.metric: Internal documentation (e.g.,  access control management document)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that the development environment has been secured, including trust access controls. This can include:
- Secure software development practices
- Monitor Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) associated with the software used
- Secure firmware and OS
- Access controls following the principle of least privilege.
- Access logging and monitoring

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures during the Design and Development of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text: Ensure that the digital assets have been secured, including data at rest and data in transit
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that the digital assets have been secured, including data at rest and data in transit. This can include:
- Implementation of recognised IT standards, such as ISO 27001

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures during the Design and Development of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text: Ensure that changes to the model or data are tracked and stored in a consistent, human- readable format
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that changes to the model or data are tracked and stored in a consistent, human-readable format. This can include the use of relevant software that:
- Tracks which users have made changes
- Allows for review before changes to an asset are made
- Allows ‘roll back’ to a backup in case of a security incident

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures during the Design and Development of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Implement measures to mitigate attacks on the dataset (e.g., poisoning attacks)

Where possible, conduct data sanitisation to remove suspicious or irrelevant data points. Augment the dataset with new data to diversify it and make it harder for attackers to inject poison data. Store the data set securely and ensure that sensitive data is protected and anonymised. Validate the performance of the machine learning model after training to ensure that it has not been poisoned
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of details of relevant mitigation measures taken. This can include the following measures:
- Data sanitisation
- Dataset augmentation
- Secure storage of dataset
- Validation of model performance

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures during the Deployment and Monitoring of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text: Implement measures to mitigate Inference Attacks, Extraction Attacks, or equivalent
all_checks_flat.metric: Internal documentation (e.g., log, register or database)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of relevant mitigation measures taken, including:

- Monitoring for API calls and/or input queries
- Internal limits on the number of queries allowed from the same IP or with similar inputs
- Implementation of secure authentication and access controls to mitigate inference attacks

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures during the Deployment and Monitoring of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text: Implement an alert system for anomalous behaviour (e.g., unathorised access)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of measures taken, including:
- Following appropriate guidance when applying logging and auditing logs
- Reporting to the relevant stakeholders and authority when an alert has been raised or an investigation has concluded that a cyber incident has occurred
- Using human-in-the-loop to investigate what automated processes flag as unusual

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures during the Deployment and Monitoring of AI system development
all_checks_flat.process_text: Develop a vulnerability disclosure process for AI system and organisation. This will allow users to report vulnerabilities in a responsible way
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence that a vulnerability disclosure process for AI system and organisation is developed, such as using UK NCSC Vulnerability Disclosure Toolkit

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures for the Continual / Online Learning Model
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Ensure that risks associated with continuous learning have been considered (e.g., poisoning attack, model/concept drift)

Determine if continual learning is still justified with the proper risk mitigations implemented
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of
- Internal approval of pre-determined model performance targets
- Continual learning model having achieved pre-determined performance targets before going into production

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures for the Continual / Online Learning Model
all_checks_flat.process_text: Ensure that approved, pre-determined performance targets are achieved before a newly updated continual learning model goes into production
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of
- Internal approval of pre-determined model performance targets
- Continual learning model having achieved pre-determined performance targets before going into production

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place security measures for End of Life of AI System
all_checks_flat.process_text: Ensure proper and secure disposal/disclosure/destruction of data and model in accordance with data privacy standards and/or relevant rules and regulations
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of proper and secure disposal/disclosure/destruction of data and model. This can include adherence to relevant standards, guidelines, rules, and regulations

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place measures to ensure the quality of data used to develop the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text:
- Implement measures to ensure data is up-to-date, complete, and representative of the environment the system will be deployed in
- Log training run metadata to do comparison in production, e.g., parameters, and version model to monitor model staleness
- Monitor production versus training data characteristics at production stage e.g., statistical distribution, data types, and validation constraints, to detect data and concept drift
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Evidence of measures implemented that documents:
- Performance metrics (e.g., accuracy, AUROC, AUPR)
- Prediction confidence score, variation ratio for the original prediction, predictive entropy
- That data is of high quality, up-to-date, complete, and representative of the environment the system will be deployed in

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Review factors that may lead to a low level of accuracy of the AI system and assess if it can result in critical, adversarial, or damaging consequences
all_checks_flat.process_text: Document intended use cases, risks, and limitations (e.g., in model cards)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of intended use cases, risks, and limitations in model cards

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Consider whether the AI system's operation can invalidate the data or assumptions it was trained on e.g., feedback loops, user adaptation, and adversarial attacks
all_checks_flat.process_text: Document intended use cases, risks, limitations (e.g., in model cards)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of intended use cases, risks, and limitations in model cards (e.g., in model cards)

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place a mechanism to evaluate when the AI system has been changed to merit a new review of its technical robustness
all_checks_flat.process_text: Implement a review process that highlights changes in code (e.g., training, serving), input data (e.g., raw data, features), and output data (e.g., inference results, performance metrics)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of mechanism to evaluate when an AI system has been changed to merit a new review of its technical robustness

Mechanism should include a review process that highlights changes in:
- code (training, serving);
- input data (e.g., raw data, features); and
- output data ( e.g.,inference results, performance metrics)

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a strategy to monitor and mitigate the risk of black box attacks on live AI systems
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Implement methods to mitigate known adversarial attacks at training time, including decisions whether to adopt / not adopt the methods.

Note: It may not be possible for all models (e.g., if the model is deterministic or with a model with high level of interactivty with users)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of implementing methods to mitigate adversarial attacks at training time, including decisions on whether to adopt / not adopt the methods

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a strategy to monitor and mitigate the risk of black box attacks on live AI systems
all_checks_flat.process_text: Monitor requests made to live AI system, e.g., frequency and feature distribution of queries, in order to detect whether it is being used suspiciously
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of monitoring requests made to live AI system, e.g, frequency and feature distribution of queries, in order to detect whether it is being used suspiciously

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a strategy to monitor and mitigate the risk of black box attacks on live AI systems
all_checks_flat.process_text: Take action on users who exhibit suspicious activity, e.g., flag for review, rate-limit or block further requests, suspend user accounts
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of action taken on users who exhibit suspicious activity.

Possible actions include to:
- flag for review;
- rate-limit or block further requests; and
- suspend user accounts

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Assess within-group fairness (also known as individual fairness)
all_checks_flat.process_text: Apply counterfactual fairness assessment
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of counterfactual fairness assessment

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place processes to test for potential biases during the entire lifecycle of the AI system, so that practitioners can act to mitigate biases based on feedback (e.g., biases due to possible limitations stemming from the composition of the used data sets such as a lack of diversity and non-representativeness)
all_checks_flat.process_text: Monitor the changes in fairness metric values in the lifecycle of the AI system.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of implemented processes to test for potential biases during the entire lifecycle of the AI system

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a strategy for the selection of fairness metrics that are aligned with the desired outcomes of the AI system's intended application
all_checks_flat.process_text: Consider using Fairness Decision Tree (e.g., AI Verify, Aequitas)  to select the appropriate metric(s) for your application
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of strategy/process undertaken to select fairness metrics that align with the desired outcomes of the AI system's intended application. For example, Binary and Multiclass Classification
- Equal Parity
- Disparate Impact
- False Negative Rate Parity
- False Positive Rate Parity
- False Omission Rate Parity
- False Discovery Rate Parity
- True Positive Rate Parity
- True Negative Rate Parity
- Negative Predictive Value Parity
- Positive Predictive Value Parity

Regression
- Mean Absolute Error Parity
- Mean Square Error Parity

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Define sensitive features for the organisation that are consistent with the legislation and corporate values
all_checks_flat.process_text: Identify the sensitive features and their privileged and unprivileged groups where feasible.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of identification of sensitive features and its privileged and unprivileged groups. Examples of sensitive features could include religion, nationality, birthplace, gender, and race. Also refer to country-specific guidelines e.g., Singapore's Tripartite Guidelines on Fair Employment Practices and UK Equality Act

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Define sensitive features for the organisation that are consistent with the legislation and corporate values
all_checks_flat.process_text: Where feasible, consult the impacted communities on the correct definition of fairness (e.g., representatives of elderly persons or persons with disabilities),  values and considerations of those impacted (e.g., individual's preference)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of consultations conducted with impacted communities on the correct definition of fairness

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a process for identifying and selecting sub-populations between which the AI system should produce fair outcomes
all_checks_flat.process_text: Define this partitioning in terms of sensitive features that models should be prohibited from being trained on, but are used in the evaluation of fairness outcomes.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of the establishment of a process for identifying and selecting sub-populations between which the AI system should produce fair outcomes

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a strategy or a set of procedures to check that the data used in the training of the AI model, is representative of the population who make up the end-users of the AI model
all_checks_flat.process_text: Perform exploratory data analysis. For the sensitive feature, test the representation of each group in the data. Resample data or collect more data if a particular group is severely underrepresented.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of the establishment of a strategy or a set of procedures to check that the data used in the training of the AI model, is representative of the population who make up the end-users of the AI model

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place a mechanism that allows for the flagging of issues related to bias, discrimination, or poor performance of the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text: Monitor threshold violations of fairness metrics post-deployment and for actual harms
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of
- monitoring of threshold violations of fairness metrics
- obtaining feedback from those impacted by the AI system, offering redress and remediation option if feasible

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place appropriate mechanisms to ensure fairness in your AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text: Monitor metrics for the latest set of data for the model currently being deployed on an ongoing basis.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of monitoring metrics for the latest set of data for the model currently being deployed on an ongoing basis

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Address the risk of biases due to possible limitations stemming from the composition of the used data sets (lack of diversity, non-representativeness), by applying appropriate adjustments on data samples of minorities
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Where possible, handle imbalanced training sets with minorities. Examples:
- Oversample minority class
- Undersample majority class
- Generate synthetic samples (SMOTE)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of addressing the risk of biases due to possible limitations stemming from the composition of the used data sets (lack of diversity, non-representativeness), by applying appropriate adjustments on data samples of minorities

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place measures to ensure data quality over time
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Verify the quality of data used in the AI system. This may include the following:
- accuracy in terms of how well the values in the dataset match the true characteristics of the entity described by the dataset
- completeness in terms of attributes and items e.g., checking for missing values, duplicate records
- veracity in terms of how credible the data is, including whether the data originated from a reliable source
- How recently the dataset was compiled or updated
- Relevance for the intended purpose
- Integrity in terms of how well extraction and transformation have been performed if multiple datasets are joined;
- Usability in terms of how the data are tracked and stored in a consistent, human-readable format
- Providing distribution analysis e.g., feature distributions of input data
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that proves due diligence has been done to ensure the quality of data. This can include the use of relevant processes or software that:
- Conducts validation schema checks
- Identifies possible errors and inconsistencies at the exploratory data analysis stage before training the dataset
- Assigns roles to the entire data pipeline to trace who manipulated data and by which rule
- Allows for review before a change is made
- Unit tests to validate that each data operation is performed correctly prior to deployment
- Allow for periodic reviewing and update of datasets
- Allow for continuous assessment of the quality of the input data to the AI system, including drift parameters and thresholds, where applicable

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place measures to understand the lineage of data, including knowing where the data originally came from, how it was collected, curated, and moved within the organisation over time
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Maintain a data provenance record to ascertain the quality of the data based on its origin and subsequent transformation. This could include the following:
- Take steps to understand the meaning of and how data was collected
- Document data usage and related concerns.
- Ensure any data labeling is done by a representative group of labelers
- Document the procedure for assessing labels for bias
- Trace potential sources of errors
-Update data
- Attribute data to their sources
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of a data provenance record that includes the following info, where applicable:
- clear explanations of what data is used, how it is collected and why
- source of data and its labels
- who the labelers were and whether bias tests were conducted to assess if the labelled data was biased (e.g., bias assessment)
- how data is transformed over time
- risk management if the origin of data is difficult to be established

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Ensure data practices comply with relevant regulatory requirements or industry standards
all_checks_flat.process_text: Ensure that assessment has been carried out in accordance with the relevant regulatory requirements and/or industry standards.  Mitigation steps have been taken.
all_checks_flat.metric: 1) Internal documentation; 2) Assessment documentation or certification(s)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that assessment has been done in accordance with the relevant data protection laws/ standards/guidelines/best practices. For example:
- applicable data protection laws and regulations such as Singapore's Personal Data Protection Act, European Data Governance Act
- Singapore's Data Protection Trustmark
- Asia Pacific Economic Cooperation Cross Border Privacy Rules and Privacy Recognition for Processors
- OECD Privacy Principles
- Recognised data governance standards from international standard bodies (e.g., ISO, US NIST, IEEE)

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Ensure team competency in data governance
all_checks_flat.process_text: Ensure that relevant team members are knowledgeable about their roles and responsibilities for data governance. Relevant team members  include any employee that is involved in managing and using the data for the AI system. For example, having a data policy team to manage the tracking of data lineage with proper controls
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that team members have relevant knowledge and training on data governance. This can include, where applicable:
- Training records
- Attendance records
- Assessments
- Certifications
- Feedback forms

all_checks_flat.criteria_text:
Ensure that the various parties involved in using, reviewing, and sponsoring the AI system are adequately trained and equipped with the necessary tools and information for proper oversight to:
- Obtain the needed information to conduct inquiries into past decisions made and actions taken throughout the AI lifecycle
- Record information on training and deploying models as part of the workflow process
all_checks_flat.process_text: Put in place guided flow for documenting (i) important info via model cards, forms, SDK library; and (ii) important processes that provide objective criteria for decision-making (e.g., fairness metrics selection)
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of guided flow for documenting:

- important info via model cards, forms, SDK library; and
- important processes that provide objective criteria for decision-making (e.g., fairness metrics selection)

all_checks_flat.criteria_text:
Ensure that the various parties involved in using, reviewing, and sponsoring the AI system are adequately trained and equipped with the necessary tools and information for proper oversight to:
- Obtain the needed information to conduct inquiries into past decisions made and actions taken throughout the AI lifecycle
- Record information on training and deploying models as part of the workflow process
all_checks_flat.process_text: Implement a data management system to gather and organise relevant information based on the needs of different user roles (e.g., reviewing models, and monitoring live systems)
all_checks_flat.metric: Internal documentation (e.g., procedure manual, log, register, or database)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of data management system to gather and organise relevant information based on the needs of different user roles

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Ensure specific oversight and control measures to reflect the self-learning or autonomous nature of the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text: Define the role of the human in its oversight and control of the AI system (e.g., human-in-the-loop, human-out-the-loop, human-over-the-loop)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of the definition of the role of human in oversight and control of the AI system

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Ensure specific oversight and control measures to reflect the self-learning or autonomous nature of the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text: When the AI model is making a decision for which it is significantly unsure of the answer/prediction, consider designing the system to be able to flag these cases and triage them for a human to review.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of consideration made in the design of the AI system on its ability to flag instances when it is making a decision for which it is significantly unsure of the answer/prediction, in order that such cases be triaged for a human to review

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Ensure specific oversight and control measures to reflect the self-learning or autonomous nature of the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text: Implement mechanisms to detect if model input represents an outlier in terms of training data (e.g., return some "data outlier score" with predictions)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of implementation of mechanisms to detect if model input represents an outlier in terms of training data

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Put in place a review process before AI models are put into production, where key features and properties of the AI model are shared and visualised in a way that is accessible to decision-makers within the organisation
all_checks_flat.process_text: Implement a systematic review process to present performance, explainability, and fairness metrics in a way that is understandable by data science, product, legal and risk, experience research, and ethics teams
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of the implementation of a systematic review process to present performance, explainability, and fairness metrics in a way that is understandable by relevant teams (e.g., data science, product, legal and risk, experience research, and ethics teams)

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a frequency and process for testing and re-evaluating AI systems
all_checks_flat.process_text:
After models are put into production, put in place mechanisms to review the performance of the models on an ongoing basis, either continuously or at regular intervals.
Criteria could be time-based (e.g., every 2 years) or event-based (before the launch of a new AI product, after the introduction of new data, operating context has changed due to external circumstances), or when the AI system has undergone substantial modification.
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of the establishment of a frequency and process for testing and re-evaluating AI systems

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Ensure the appropriate parties who are accountable for the AI system (e.g., AI governance committee, AI system owner, and reviewers) have considered how the AI system is used to benefit humans in decision-making processes
all_checks_flat.process_text: Declaration of transparency on how and where in the decision-making process the AI system is used to complement or replace the human.
all_checks_flat.metric: 1) Internal documentation (e.g., procedure manual) 2) External / internal correspondence
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of the declaration of transparency on how and where in the decision-making process the AI system is used to complement or replace the human

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Ensure that the development of AI system is for the beneficial outcomes for individuals, society, and the environment
all_checks_flat.process_text: Put in place a process to determine that the development and deployment of the AI system is for the benefit of people, society, and the environment, where applicable
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of consideration of AI system's impact on individuals, society, and environment, which may include (where applicable):
- Human capabilities to learn and make decisions
- Skills, jobs, and/or job quality
- Creative economies
- Discriminatory and/or exclusionary norms
- Environmental concerns

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish clear internal governance mechanisms to ensure clear roles and responsibilities for the use of AI by the organisation
all_checks_flat.process_text: Adapt existing structures, communication lines, procedures, and rules (e.g., three lines of defense risk management model) or implement new ones
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of adaptation or new implementation of structures, communication lines, procedures, and rules (e.g., three lines of defense risk management model)

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish clear internal governance mechanisms to ensure clear roles and responsibilities for the use of AI by the organisation
all_checks_flat.process_text: For organisations who are using AI across departments, establish an AI governance committee that comprises representatives from data science, technology, risk, and product to facilitate cross-departmental oversight for the lifecycle governance of AI systems
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of the establishment of an AI governance committee.

This committee should be sufficiently representative. One way to achieve this is by having representatives from:
- data science;
- technology;
- legal and compliance;
- risk and product; and
- user experience research, ethics, and psychology

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish clear internal governance mechanisms to ensure clear roles and responsibilities for the use of AI by the organisation
all_checks_flat.process_text: Enable a process to report on actions or decisions that affect the AI system's outcome, and a corresponding process for the accountable party to respond to the consequences of such an outcome
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence that outlines roles, responsibilities, and key processes for

- the reporting on actions or decisions that affect the AI system's outcome;
- the corresponding process for the accountable party to respond to the consequences of such an outcome

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish the appropriate process or governance-by-design technology to automate or facilitate the AI system’s auditability throughout its lifecycle
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Process or technology should handle:
- Version control of code and model
- Version data or maintain immutable data
- Audit trail of deployment history, log inputs/outputs, associate server predictions with the originating model
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of the establishment of the appropriate process or governance-by-design technology to automate or facilitate the AI system’s auditability throughout its lifecycle.

The process or technology should handle:
- Version control of code and model;
- Version data or maintain immutable data; and
- Audit trail of deployment history, log inputs/outputs, associate server predictions with the originating model

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Define the policy mechanism for enforcing access rights and permissions for the various roles of users
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Implement fine-grained access control that aligns with various roles for users:
- Access to code and data for training AI models
- Access to code and data for deploying AI models
- Access to different execution environments
- Permission to perform various actions (e.g., launch training job, review model, deploy model server)
- Permission to define access control rules and perform other administrative functions
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of the implementation of fine-grained access control that aligns with various roles for users, which include:

- Access to code and data for training AI models
- Access to code and data for deploying AI models
- Access to different execution environments
- Permission to perform various actions (e.g., launch training job, review model, deploy model server)
- Permission to define access control rules and perform other administrative functions

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish a strategy for maintaining independent oversight over the development and deployment of AI systems
all_checks_flat.process_text: Reviewers should be distinct from those who are training and deploying models. However, it is acceptable to have the same individuals training and deploying models
all_checks_flat.metric: Internal documentation (e.g., log, register or database)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of strategy for maintaining independent oversight over the development and deployment of AI systems

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: If you are using third-party ‘black box’ models, assess the suitability and limits of the model for your use case
all_checks_flat.process_text: Evaluate the necessity of third-party models e.g., they are trained on data otherwise not accessible to your organisation ,or you do not have the requisite capability to build AI systems in-house
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of evaluation completed regarding the necessity of third-party models

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: If you are using third-party ‘black box’ models, assess the suitability and limits of the model for your use case
all_checks_flat.process_text: Demonstrate effort to understand how the third-party models were built, including 1) what data was used to train the models, 2) how the models are assessed for effectiveness and explainability 3) under what circumstances does the AI system perform poorly
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of effort undertaken to understand how the third-party models were built, which includes:

- what data was used to train the models;
- how the models are assessed for effectiveness and explainability; and
- under what circumstances does the AI system perform poorly

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish clear responsibilities between different parties within the broader supply chain – partners, suppliers, customers, third parties
all_checks_flat.process_text: Responsibilities and obligations are clearly communicated to all parties related to the AI system
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of communication with relevant parties on responsibilities and obligations relating to the AI system

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Establish processes to ensure that its usage of services, products or materials are in support of the responsible development and use of the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text:
Select AI system suppliers which align with organisation’s approach.

When AI system suppliers do not perform as intended, there are processes for suppliers to take remedial actions.
all_checks_flat.check_description:
Documentary evidence of processes to select suppliers which align with the organisation’s approach.

Documentary evidence of correspondence with suppliers to take corrective action.

all_checks_flat.criteria_text: Incorporate end users’ expectations/needs in the responsible development and use of the AI system
all_checks_flat.process_text: Process to engage end users on their expectations and needs (e.g. providing general usage agreements that scope its use)
all_checks_flat.check_description: Documentary evidence of attempts to understand end users needs and mitigate risks related to its misuse
